factors.summary: The company's pricing power is mixed. While cash flows from operations remain positive, indicating some stability, pricing power is challenged in international markets due to U.S. dollar strength and lower fuel surcharges.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin domestic segments and away from lower-margin international segments. This shift has a mixed impact on overall margins due to factors such as economic challenges and currency fluctuations.

evidence.text: Revenues related to our international regions decreased 9.5% year-over-year primarily due to the continued strength of the U.S. dollar, reductions in international fuel surcharges and economic challenges in certain regions.

Our capital expenditures were $871 million and $586 million for the three months ended March 31, 2016 and 2015, respectively. We expect to invest approximately $3 billion in 2016 primarily for (1) aircraft, including deliveries of B-737-900ERs, A321-200s, A330-300s and E190-100s, along with advance deposit payments for these and our A330-900neo and A350-900 orders, as well as for (2) aircraft modifications, the majority of which relate to increasing the seat density and enhancing the cabins on our domestic fleet.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shifting towards higher-margin domestic segments with mixed impacts.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost reduction initiatives, primarily through capacity actions and fleet restructuring, which have resulted in some savings. evidence.text: Cost reduction initiatives, such as capacity actions and fleet restructuring, are delivering savings, though not enough to offset all margin pressures.
